post.summary:
RegisterOAuthProvider registers an OAuth application with the
backend. Admin-only.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
RegisterOAuthProviderRequest is the request message for
RegisterOAuthProvider. Admin-only. Registers an OAuth application
with the backend so users may subsequently authorize it.

The `provider_type` is a backend-interpreted string identifying
the upstream OAuth provider — for example `"github"`, `"linear"`,
`"atlassian"`, `"google"`. The implementation's OAuth plugin
resolves authorize / token / refresh URLs from this string.

delete.summary:
DeleteOAuthCredential removes a per-user OAuth grant from the
backend. Does not revoke the grant upstream at the provider.

parameters.name: providerName
parameters.description: Name of the registered OAuth provider.

parameters.name: providerName
parameters.description: Name of the registered OAuth provider.

post.summary:
GetOAuthAuthorizeURL returns the URL to redirect the user to in
order to start the authorization-code flow.

parameters.name: providerName
parameters.description: Name of a previously registered OAuth provider.

post.summary:
ExchangeOAuthCode exchanges an authorization code for tokens
and stores them under the per-user convention. Called by the
OAuth callback handler.

parameters.name: providerName
parameters.description: Name of the registered OAuth provider.

userId.description:
User whose flow this completes. Implementations verify this
matches the user encoded in `state`.

code.description: Authorization code from the provider's callback.

state.description:
State token returned by GetOAuthAuthorizeURL. Must match to
prevent CSRF.

redirectUrl.description: The same redirect URL passed to GetOAuthAuthorizeURL.

AuthExchangeOAuthCodeBody.description:
ExchangeOAuthCodeRequest is the request message for
ExchangeOAuthCode. Exchanges the authorization code returned by
the provider for access + refresh tokens; stores them in the
backend under the per-user OAuth credential convention.

redirectUrl.description: URL the provider will redirect back to after authorization.

scopes.description:
Scopes to request from the provider. Empty means use the
backend's default scopes for this provider type.

AuthGetOAuthAuthorizeURLBody.description:
GetOAuthAuthorizeURLRequest is the request message for
GetOAuthAuthorizeURL. Returns the URL the caller should redirect
the user's browser to in order to start the authorization-code
flow.

v1DeleteOAuthCredentialResponse.description:
DeleteOAuthCredentialResponse is the response message for
DeleteOAuthCredential. It is intentionally empty.

v1ExchangeOAuthCodeResponse.description:
ExchangeOAuthCodeResponse is the response message for
ExchangeOAuthCode. It is intentionally empty — the per-user
credential is now stored in the backend and is reachable via
GetOAuthToken.

authorizeUrl.description: URL the caller should redirect the user's browser to.

state.description:
Opaque state token the caller MUST pass back to
ExchangeOAuthCode to prove the callback corresponds to this
flow. Implementations typically encode user_id and a nonce.

v1GetOAuthAuthorizeURLResponse.description:
GetOAuthAuthorizeURLResponse is the response message for
GetOAuthAuthorizeURL.

v1GetOAuthTokenResponse.description:
GetOAuthTokenResponse is the response message for GetOAuthToken.
The backend MUST transparently refresh the underlying grant before
returning so the returned `access_token` is usable for the
provider's full access-token TTL window.

accessToken.description:
The access token the caller sends to the upstream MCP server as
`Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

expiresAt.description:
When `access_token` becomes invalid if not refreshed. The
dispenser SHOULD refresh before this time on the next
GetOAuthToken call; callers should treat it as informational.

v1OAuthToken.description:
OAuthToken is a per-user OAuth 2.0 access token materialized by the
OAuth dispenser. The dispenser auto-refreshes the underlying grant
as needed; callers see a fresh `access_token` on every read.

name.description:
Display name of the provider registration. Used by subsequent
dispenser RPCs to identify which provider to act on. Unique
within the backend.

clientId.description: OAuth client ID issued by the upstream provider.

clientSecret.description:
OAuth client secret issued by the upstream provider. Stored
encrypted in the backend; never returned.

v1RegisterOAuthProviderRequest.description:
RegisterOAuthProviderRequest is the request message for
RegisterOAuthProvider. Admin-only. Registers an OAuth application
with the backend so users may subsequently authorize it.

The `provider_type` is a backend-interpreted string identifying
the upstream OAuth provider — for example `"github"`, `"linear"`,
`"atlassian"`, `"google"`. The implementation's OAuth plugin
resolves authorize / token / refresh URLs from this string.

v1RegisterOAuthProviderResponse.description:
RegisterOAuthProviderResponse is the response message for
RegisterOAuthProvider. It is intentionally empty — the registration
is identified on subsequent calls by `name`.
